title: veronica spuria var. cartilaginea
intro: Veronica spuria var. cartilaginea, also known as cartilaginous broomrape, is a plant species within the broomrape family (Orobanchaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including relief from pain, fever reduction, and the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Veronica spuria var. cartilaginea can be used as a tincture or infused in water to create a medicinal tea.
